sections.paragraph:
constr. חַטַּאת plur. חַטָּאֹות f. [“a miss, misstep, slip with the foot, Pro 13:6 ”].

(1) sin, Exo 28:9 Isa 6:7, etc. [“Rarely for the habit of sinning, sinfulness, Pro 14:34 Isa 3:9.”] Also applied to that by which any one sins, e.g. idols, Hos 10:8 Deu 9:21 comp. 2Ki 13:2, water of sin, i.e. of expiation or purifying, Num 8:7.

(2) a sin offering, Levit. 6:18 , 23 as to its difference from אָשָׁם see that word.

(3) penalty, Lam 3:39 Zec 14:19 hence calamity, misfortune, Isa 40:2 Pro 10:16 (opp. to חַיִּים). [Is not this last sense wholly needless? and would not its introduction utterly mar the sense of the passages referred to in support of it?]
